evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
Entity of type FAQPage has no @id — cross-page interconnection blocked
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~14 FAQ pairs
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice vs committee 4/10 — Mostly committee-style marketing copy, though occasional zingers break through.
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — A few sharp opinions appear (coaching-as-theatre, willpower line), but they're buried in safe hedged prose.
Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — Some memorable turns ('overpriced theatre', '3pm energy crash'), but surrounded by 'leveraging a structured...clinically grounded reset' boilerplate.
Point of view vs both-sides survey 6/10 — Has some POV ('Skip the coaching upsell') but mostly presents as neutral guidance.
Quotable sentences 6/10 — One or two quotable lines exist, but they're buried under conventional prose.
Intro earns attention 5/10 — The actual strong hook ('Fifteen minutes with a pen can flip that') is buried under meta-headers, TL;DR, bylines, and repeated stats.

answer_extractability 6/10 — The primary keyword answer is scattered across a TL;DR block, a 'Quick answer' box, and repeated body paragraphs. No single 40–60 word passage cleanly states: what a Q1 health check is, what it produces, and what UAE adults should specifically do. An AI assistant would need to stitch together three separate sections. A single consolidated answer block placed immediately after the headline would fix extractability.
